headline: Chevron’s Middle East Shift Puts Geopolitical Risk At Center Stage
summary: Chevron temporarily shut the Leviathan gas field in Israel after government orders linked to rising military conflict involving the US, Israel, and Iran. The shutdown directly affects Chevron’s regional gas operations and exposes the company to heightened geopolitical and supply risk. At the same time, Chevron has secured new oil contracts in Iraq, including roles in fields such as West Qurna 2, following the exit of Russian firms. For investors tracking NYSE:CVX, these developments put...

headline: Commodity Strategist: Oil Would Be in the $40s Without Iran War
summary: Carley Garner, senior commodity market strategist at Carley Trading, has a take on oil that should make energy bulls nervous: strip out the Iran conflict, and crude would be trading significantly lower right now, in her view. That’s a striking claim when you look at where prices actually are. Brent crude has been trading in ... Commodity Strategist: Oil Would Be in the $40s Without Iran War

headline: Liquefied Natural Gas (LNG) Infrastructure Industry Report 2026-2035: A $138.43 Billion Market by 2030 with ExxonMobil, Chevron, BP, Shell, TotalEnergies, Cheniere Energy, QatarEnergy Leading
summary: The LNG infrastructure market is driven by investments in onshore storage, liquefaction plants, and pipelines, along with marine transport and heavy-duty vehicle use. Future opportunities include floating regasification, modular plants, and LNG distribution expansion, spurred by electricity demand and transport adoption. headline: Bank of America resets Chevron stock price target for 2026
summary: Wall Street is turning more bullish on Chevron. Bank of America raised its price target on the energy giant to $206 from $188 on Monday, keeping a Buy rating in place as tensions in the Middle East show little sign of cooling. Shares responded fast. Chevron hit a fresh one-year high of $191.44 on ...
